summary:
The mandibular second premolar has a larger crown volume distally compared to its mesial portion due to prominent facial and lingual contour development. This makes it particularly prone to exhibiting significant crown bulk beyond the faciolingual dissecting plane when considering dental anatomy patterns related to occlusal morphology.

The mandibular second premolar shows marked crown bulk distal to the faciolingual dissecting plane due to pronounced facial and lingual contours. This feature distinguishes it from other teeth and aligns with known anatomic variations affecting proximal-occlusal relationships in posterior dentition.
